tiers.1: Tolkien himself, and Christopher Tolkien's editions
tiers.2: published with the approval of the Tolkien Estate
tiers.3: in harmony with Tolkien, but not of his hand
tiers.4: outside the canon; used only where Tolkien would own the claim

sources.note: The constructed Silmarillion. Authoritative, but a made book: where it differs from the late texts in Morgoth's Ring and The War of the Jewels, the later manuscript states Tolkien's final intent and the published text states his son's editorial choice.

sources.file: (B) The Hobbit, or There and Back Again.pdf
sources.note: Published in his lifetime and revised by him. Highest authority for its own matter.
With Appendices A-F. The single densest authority for the Third Age, the calendars, the languages and the writing-systems.

sources.file: (D) Unfinished Tales of Númenor and Middle-earth.pdf
sources.note: Late narratives with editorial commentary. Often supersedes The Silmarillion in detail.
The tale in its layers, from the Lost Tales to the late prose.
The Narn assembled as a continuous narrative.
Every version of the tale, including the Lost Tale with the Twelve Houses.

sources.file: 1 The Book of Lost Tales, Part One.pdf
sources.note: The earliest mythology. Names, cosmology and even characters differ from the late work; authoritative for what Tolkien first wrote, not for what he last held.

sources.file: 2 The Book of Lost Tales, Part Two.pdf
sources.note: Contains the Fall of Gondolin with the fullest account of the Twelve Houses and their arms.
The long verse: the Lay of the Children of Húrin and the Lay of Leithian.
Sketch, Quenta, earliest Annals, the first maps and the Ambarkanta cosmology.

sources.file: 5 The Lost Road and Other Writings.pdf
sources.note: The Lhammas and the Etymologies — the deepest single source for the roots of the tongues.
Drafts of The Lord of the Rings, I.
Drafts II; the first LotR map; the runes.
Drafts III.
Drafts IV; the Notion Club Papers; the Adûnaic material.
The later Silmarillion I: Annals of Aman, the Athrabeth, and Myths Transformed — where Tolkien reconsidered the shape of the world itself.
The later Silmarillion II: the Grey Annals, Quendi and Eldar, the late Beleriand matter.
The Appendices' evolution, the Shibboleth of Fëanor, Of Dwarves and Men, the last writings.

sources.attribution: HoME XIII: Index, ed. Christopher Tolkien
sources.note: Master index to the History. A finding-aid, not a source of claims.

sources.file: The Adventures of Tom Bombadil and Other Verses from the Red Book.pdf
sources.note: Tolkien's verse, with his own framing preface placing the poems in the Red Book.

sources.file: The Collected Poems of J.R.R. Tolkien.pdf
sources.note: The gathered verse. Tolkien's own words throughout; the apparatus is the editors'.

sources.file: secondary sources/The Letters of J.R.R. Tolkien.pdf
sources.attribution: ed. Humphrey Carpenter with Christopher Tolkien (1981; expanded 2023)
sources.note: Tolkien in his own voice on his own work — the primary authority for intent, and for questions the fiction leaves open. Was filed under secondary sources; it is not secondary.
Collects Tolkien's own shorter works, including On Fairy-Stories, The Homecoming of Beorhtnoth, Leaf by Niggle and Farmer Giles of Ham.

sources.file: secondary sources/J. R. R. Tolkien- Artist and Illustrator
sources.attribution: Hammond & Scull (1995) — reproducing J.R.R. Tolkien's own art
sources.note: The pictures are Tolkien's and carry primary authority, including the 1960 heraldic devices and his written note on the rules of Elvish heraldry. The surrounding commentary is Hammond and Scull's and is weighed at tier 2. The images are (c) the Tolkien Estate and are not reproduced by this archive.
Same text as (B); held for its plates.

sources.file: (H) The Fall of Númenor and Other Tales from the Second Age of Middle-earth.pdf
sources.note: An Estate-approved gathering of Tolkien's Second-Age writing. The quoted Tolkien text carries tier-1 authority; the arrangement, headings and linking commentary are the editor's.
Estate-approved publication of Tolkien's late essays on time, ageing, body and spirit, and the lands and peoples. The essays are Tolkien's and carry tier-1 authority.

sources.file: The J. R. R. Tolkien Companion and Guide.pdf
sources.note: The authorized Chronology and Reader's Guide. Superb for publication history and for what Tolkien wrote when; its interpretations are still interpretation.

sources.file: The Lord of the Rings A Reader_s Companion.pdf
sources.note: Line-by-line commentary on The Lord of the Rings, and the fullest text of Tolkien's own Nomenclature — his instructions to translators, which is primary matter.

sources.file: secondary sources/The Art of The Fellowship of the Ring.pdf
sources.note: Licensed film concept art. Authoritative for the films, never for Middle-earth.
The standard cartographic reconstruction. Careful and honest about its own inference, but its distances and coastlines are Fonstad's reasoning, not Tolkien's measurement.
A reliable concordance to the published works. Predates the History, so silent on much.
A fuller encyclopaedia, looser than Foster and occasionally speculative without saying so.
A grammar of Sindarin reconstructed from Tolkien's scattered material. Scholarly, but it regularises where Tolkien never settled; its paradigms must not be quoted as his.

sources.attribution: A. Żurek, 'Heraldry in J.R.R. Tolkien's Legendarium', The Coat of Arms 4th ser. 5 (2022)
sources.note: Peer-reviewed heraldic scholarship; the source for this archive's heraldic reconstructions. Its figures are reserved by their originators and are not reproduced here.
Held for reference only. Provenance unverified, so nothing is asserted from it.
Game mechanics and invented detail. Nothing here is evidence about Middle-earth; usable only where it happens to restate something Tolkien wrote.

sources.file: secondary sources/Reader And Educator Guide To the Hobbit And the Lord Of The Rings.pdf
sources.note: Pedagogical material. No independent authority.
Useful only as evidence that particular manuscripts and editions exist.

not_held.title: The Letters of J.R.R. Tolkien — Revised and Expanded Edition (2023)
not_held.attribution: ed. Carpenter & C. Tolkien; revised Hammond & Scull
not_held.note: The copy held is the 1981 edition, ending at letter 354. The 2023 revision restores cut passages and adds roughly 150 further letters.
not_held.affects: Any claim resting on Tolkien's stated intent. The archive should cite the 1981 numbering and not assert that a subject is absent from the Letters.

not_held.title: The Monsters and the Critics and Other Essays (1983)
not_held.note: Tolkien's scholarly essays, including 'On Fairy-Stories', 'English and Welsh' and 'A Secret Vice' — his own account of why and how he invented languages.
not_held.affects: The languages hall, and any statement about Tolkien's philosophy of invention.

not_held.title: The Bovadium Fragments (2025)
not_held.attribution: ed. Christopher Tolkien; essay by Richard Ovenden
not_held.note: A previously unpublished satire on the motor-car and the ruin of Oxford, and the last of Christopher Tolkien's presentations of his father's work. No Middle-earth matter.
not_held.affects: Nothing in this archive. Registered for completeness of the tier-1 shelf.

not_held.title: The Fall of Arthur (2013); The Legend of Sigurd and Gudrún (2009); Beowulf: A Translation and Commentary (2014); The Story of Kullervo (2015); The Lay of Aotrou and Itroun (2016)
not_held.note: Tolkien's verse and translations outside the legendarium.
not_held.affects: Nothing directly; useful only for the sources of his imagination.

not_held.title: Roverandom; Mr Bliss; Letters from Father Christmas; Smith of Wootton Major
not_held.note: His other fiction. Smith touches Faery, but none is Middle-earth.

not_held.title: Parma Eldalamberon 11–22 (1995– )
not_held.attribution: ed. Elvish Linguistic Fellowship, by permission of the Tolkien Estate and with Christopher Tolkien's support
not_held.note: The single largest body of primary Tolkien not held here: his own language manuscripts printed in full — the Gnomish Lexicon (PE 11), the Qenya Lexicon (PE 12), Early Noldorin (PE 13), 'Words, Phrases and Passages' from The Lord of the Rings (PE 17), the Quenya phonology and verb structure (PE 18, 19, 22). The text is Tolkien's and carries tier-1 authority; the journals are the Estate-permitted vehicle.
not_held.affects: The languages hall above all. Its lexicon is built from the Etymologies (HoME V) and the published appendices; PE 17 and PE 22 would correct and greatly enlarge it. Until these are read, no entry in that hall should be presented as the whole of what Tolkien wrote.

not_held.title: Vinyar Tengwar, esp. nos. 39–50
not_held.attribution: ed. Elvish Linguistic Fellowship, by Estate permission
not_held.note: Tolkien's late essays: 'Ósanwe-kenta' on thought-transfer, 'Notes on Óre' on the inner heart, 'The Rivers and Beacon-hills of Gondor', and the Quenya prayers.
not_held.affects: The languages hall; also the cosmology and any statement about Elvish mind and spirit.

not_held.title: The Art of The Hobbit (2011); The Art of The Lord of the Rings (2015)
not_held.note: Tolkien's own drawings, maps and designs for the two books, reproduced in full.
not_held.affects: The gallery and the maps. His own cartography outranks any reconstruction the archive draws.

not_held.title: Tolkien: Maker of Middle-earth (2018)
not_held.note: The exhibition catalogue: manuscripts, letters and artwork, including heraldic devices.

not_held.title: The History of The Hobbit (2007; rev. 2011)
not_held.note: The drafts of The Hobbit with commentary, the counterpart to Christopher Tolkien's History for the one book he never covered.
not_held.affects: The Hobbit-era chronology and the dwarves.

rule: Where two sources disagree, the lower tier number wins. A tier-5 file is corrected by any tier above it. Tolkien's own words quoted inside a tier-2 book carry tier-1 authority; the editor's framing does not.

limits.what: The genealogical tables of Appendix A III (Durin's Folk) and Appendix C (the hobbit trees)
limits.why: In the edition held here these tables are embedded as IMAGES, not text, so no text extraction reaches them. They have now been recovered by rendering the pages and reading the plates directly, and cross-checked with tesseract OCR.
limits.effect: RESOLVED. The Dwarf line is verified against App A III: six birth-years were wrong in this archive and are corrected (Nain I was out by 101 years). The hobbit dates are verified against App C and were correct in every case checked, but now carry the warrant they lacked. Where OCR and a direct reading disagreed, the reading was confirmed at 450 dpi and OCR was found to be the one in error.
